title: zanthoxylum beecheyanum var. alatum
intro: Zanthoxylum beecheyanum var. alatum, also known as Taiwanese prickly ash, is a plant species belonging to the Rutaceae family, characterized by its unique, thorny branches and small, yellowish flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, as well as its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve respiratory issues. Taiwanese prickly ash is also believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating nausea, and protecting against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant's bark and leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
